skill.name: hypothesis-generation
skill.description: Generate testable scientific hypotheses from observations, literature evidence, competing explanations, predictions, and experimental design constraints.
skill.summary: Build evidence-based hypotheses with predictions, comparisons, and experiment plans.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography hits are false positives from scientific and formatting text. The real concerns are broad Bash and file-write permissions, LaTeX or Python command examples, and guided public web or PubMed access. I found no prompt-injection attempt or evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash and File Write Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill front matter permits Bash, Write, and Edit. It also shows Python and LaTeX command examples that could execute local tools if a user approves them. This is legitimate for report generation, but it increases operational risk. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for capability risk, not malicious intent. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The permission and command examples are explicit, but the reviewed content does not hide payloads or pass untrusted data into a command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission and command examples are explicit. The reviewed content does not hide payloads or pass untrusted data into a command.

medium_findings.title: External Literature and Web Access Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs use of WebFetch, WebSearch, and PubMed URLs for literature review. These are expected research actions, but they can disclose user research topics to external services. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for network risk, with legitimate purpose. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The network destinations are public research resources and search tools, with no evidence of credential or file exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network use is documented and tied to literature search. I found no evidence that secrets or local files are sent to external endpoints.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings are triggered by ordinary scientific or formatting words, such as description, design, and hypothesis. I found no cryptographic API calls, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive encryption logic in the reviewed context. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown, LaTeX, and research guidance text rather than executable cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown, LaTeX, and research guidance text. No cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Most Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts come from Markdown inline code, fenced LaTeX examples, and formatting references. They are not executed by the skill itself. The true command examples are separately covered as medium risk. Verdict: MOSTLY_FALSE_POSITIVE. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: The cited examples are documentation blocks, but some Bash examples could be copied or approved by users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples are documentation blocks. A smaller subset of Bash examples remains relevant as user-approved command risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Approved Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requests Bash permission and documents commands for schematic generation and LaTeX compilation. Users should inspect paths and inputs before running these commands. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The commands are visible and purposeful, but local execution can affect files and invoke installed tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and purposeful. Local execution can affect files and invoke installed tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Research Query Network Disclosure
dangerous_patterns.description: Literature search guidance sends research topics to PubMed or web search providers. This is expected for the skill, but sensitive research topics should be reviewed before external lookup. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The URLs and tool guidance are explicit and benign, but the user query content may be sensitive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and tool guidance are explicit and benign. The user query content may still be sensitive.

content.value_statement: Researchers often need clear hypotheses that connect observations to evidence and experiments. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured hypothesis generation and report writing.
actual_capabilities: Turns observations or research questions into three to five competing hypotheses.
Evaluates hypotheses using testability, falsifiability, parsimony, scope, consistency, and novelty criteria.
Suggests experimental designs, controls, measurements, confounds, and falsification criteria.
Guides literature searches across PubMed and general scientific sources.
Produces a structured LaTeX hypothesis report using included templates and style files.
Organizes evidence, predictions, comparisons, and appendices for scientific review.
limitations: It does not validate scientific claims without external literature review by the model or user.
It can suggest experiments, but it cannot guarantee feasibility, safety, ethics approval, or statistical power.
LaTeX compilation depends on local TeX packages and fonts being installed.
The schematic generation command references another skill and may not work unless that skill is installed.

use_cases.title: Plan a Thesis Study
use_cases.description: Convert a preliminary observation into competing hypotheses, predictions, and a first experimental plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Grant Aims
use_cases.description: Compare mechanisms, identify decisive experiments, and document evidence gaps before drafting grant aims.

use_cases.title: Review Product Research
use_cases.description: Frame technical anomalies as testable explanations with measurable outcomes and practical validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create Initial Hypotheses
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new research question
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three testable hypotheses for this observation: [observation]. Include a mechanism, one prediction, and one falsification condition for each.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Explanations
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these hypotheses for [phenomenon]. Score each for testability, parsimony, explanatory power, and consistency with known evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Design Experiments
prompt_templates.prompt: Design experiments to distinguish these hypotheses: [hypotheses]. Include controls, measurements, expected outcomes, confounds, and failure criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build Full Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise hypothesis generation report for [phenomenon]. Include literature context, competing mechanisms, predictions, critical comparisons, and appendices.

output_examples.input: Observation: treated cells recover faster than untreated cells after oxidative stress.
output_examples.output: The skill would produce competing mechanisms, such as improved antioxidant response, altered repair signaling, or selection of resistant cells. It would add predictions and experiments to separate them.

output_examples.input: Question: why does a material lose conductivity after repeated humidity cycles?
output_examples.output: The skill would frame mechanisms like surface oxidation, microcrack formation, or ion migration. It would propose measurements and controls for each mechanism.

output_examples.input: Goal: prepare a four-page hypothesis report for a lab meeting.
output_examples.output: The skill would structure a concise report with an executive summary, hypothesis boxes, key predictions, comparisons, and detailed appendices.

best_practices: Provide the core observation, domain, constraints, and any preliminary data before asking for hypotheses.
Ask for competing explanations, not only the most likely explanation.
Review literature, safety, ethics, and statistics with domain experts before using proposed experiments.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated hypotheses as established facts without evidence.
Do not skip falsification criteria or discriminating experiments.
Do not run LaTeX or schematic commands without checking local files and paths first.

faq.question: Does this skill perform the literature search automatically?
faq.answer: It gives search strategies and may guide WebFetch or WebSearch use when those tools are available.

faq.question: Can it create final scientific conclusions?
faq.answer: No. It creates hypotheses and test plans. Users must validate conclusions with evidence and expert review.

faq.question: What output format does it favor?
faq.answer: It favors a structured LaTeX report with hypothesis boxes, prediction boxes, comparisons, and appendices.

faq.question: Can it support non-biomedical research?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow applies to many domains, including materials, earth science, engineering, and computational research.

faq.question: Why does it request Bash access?
faq.answer: The skill includes examples for generating figures and compiling LaTeX reports. Users should review commands before running them.

faq.question: Does it replace statistical or ethics review?
faq.answer: No. Proposed experiments still require domain, safety, ethics, and statistical review before implementation.
